cause_of_action: OTHER CIVIL PETITIONS (FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF)

docket_entries.description: OPPOS TO NON-PARTY PAULINE HAMITON'S PETITION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER; MEMO OF P&A; DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.

DISCOVERY 302, PAULINE HAMILTON'S PETITION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE PETITION IS GOVERNED BY CALIFORNIA LAW. CCP 2029.500. MS. HAMILTON SHALL SIT FOR A DEPOSITION, BUT THE DEPOSITION SHALL NOT REQUIRE MS. HAMILTON (1) TO DISCLOSE MARITAL COMMUNICATIONS SUBJECT TO EVIDENCE CODE 980; (2) TO ANSWER QUESTIONS SOLELY ABOUT HER OWN PERSONAL FINANCIAL CONDITION; OR (3) TO ANSWER DIRECT QUESTIONS RELATING TO MS. HAMILTON'S MINOR SON OR CHILD SUPPORT PAYMENTS FOR THE MINOR'S BENEFIT. THESE LIMITATIONS WILL PROTECT THE BEST INTERESTS OF THE MINOR SON AND TO PROTECT ALL APPLICABLE RIGHTS TO PRIVACY. THE PURPOSE OF THE DEPOSITION IS TO INQUIRE INTO MR. LINDSETH'S FINANCIAL CONDITION, AND NOT THE FINANCIAL CONDITION OF MS. HAMILTON. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE DEPOSITION'S SCOPE IS LIMITED IN THIS MANNER, IT DOES NOT APPEAR TO IMPLICATE MS. HAMILTON'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY. OTHER THAN STATED IN THIS ORDER, MS. HAMILTON'S PETITION IS DENIED. ANY APPLICABLE OBJECTIONS OR ASSERTIONS OF PRIVILEGE CAN BE MADE DURING THE COURSE OF THE DEPOSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM, WITH CC: TO OTHER PARTIES BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM BY TODAY. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.=(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allan B. Felahey, Esq. of Felahy Law Group (562-499-2121) for Petitioner Pauline Hamilton.
Elios S. Blut, Esq. of Blut Law Group (310-203-0038) for Respondent High Quality Organics, Inc.

Hearing is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PAULINE HAMILTON's Petition For Protective Order is granted in part and denied in part. The petition is governed by California law. CCP 2029.500. Ms. Hamilton shall sit for a deposition, but the deposition shall not require Ms. Hamilton (1) to disclose marital communications subject to Evidence Code 980; (2) to answer questions solely about her own personal financial condition; or (3) to answer direct questions relating to Ms. Hamilton's minor son or child support payments for the minor's benefit. These limitations will protect the best interests of the minor son and to protect all applicable rights to privacy. The purpose of the deposition is to inquire into Mr. Lindseth's financial condition, and not the financial condition of Ms. Hamilton. To the extent that the deposition's scope is limited in this manner, it does not appear to implicate Ms. Hamilton's right to privacy. Other than stated in this order, Ms. Hamilton's petition is denied. Any applicable objections or assertions of privilege can be made during the course of the deposition. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form, with cc: to other parties by email to the Judge Pro tem by today. Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)
